summary:
The patient has regionally advanced squamous cell carcinoma involving cervical lymph nodes without identified primary lesion or distant metastases. Staging follows TXN2aM0 based on AJCC guidelines—accounting for unknown origin (T?), regional nodal spread (>2cm in single level [N2a]), and absent distant dissemination (M0). 

This summary meets requirements by focusing solely on essential clinical facts related to diagnosis, location, extent, and appropriate staging using accepted standards.
